TIER Wealth Calculators

CRA Tax Instalment CalculatorPlanning Hub

Choose the starting point that matches the information you have with you today, and the tool will open the right calculator pathway for review and planning.

I have my tax returns

You have your prior year tax returns or Notices of Assessment (NOA) on hand — the NOA is preferred since it shows CRA’s confirmed figures. We’ll compare all three instalment methods and recommend the lowest-cost option.

Prior Year · Second Prior Year · Current Year Estimate
→

I received a CRA instalment notice

CRA has mailed you a notice showing specific instalment amounts. Enter the figures from your letter and we’ll show your optimal payment schedule — with an optional current year comparison.

Amounts from CRA Notice
→
Not sure which applies? If you have received a letter from CRA with specific dollar amounts for March/June or September/December instalments — choose the second option.

Which notice did you receive?

CRA sends two instalment notices per year. Select the one you have in front of you.

March & June Notice

Q1March 15
Q2June 15

Typically received in February. Shows your first two instalment amounts. With the NOA from your most recently filed tax return, we can calculate Q3 and Q4 right now — giving you the complete annual picture without waiting for the August notice.

September & December Notice

Q3September 15
Q4December 15

Typically received in August. Shows your Q3 and Q4 instalment amounts. Enter your Q1/Q2 amounts from the February notice for a complete annual picture.

Are you confused because you received a March and June notice but aren’t sure why you didn’t receive a September and December notice? You’re not alone — we can help with that scenario here too. Just have the details from your March and June notice and the NOA from your most recently filed tax return on hand.

Calculator Inputs

From your tax returns
Assumptions
This is the year your income was earned — not the year you file. For example, instalments made in 2026 typically relate to your 2026 tax year.
Filing & payment deadline: CRA calculates instalment interest to April 30, 2027 for all individual filers. Self-employed individuals have until June 15 to file, but any balance owing is still due April 30, 2027.
Prior Year (2025) — Most Recently Filed Return

Enter figures from your 2025 T1 return or Notice of Assessment. Most people only have lines 43500 and 43700 — enter 0 for any line not shown.

Second Prior Year (2024)

The same figures from your 2024 T1 return or Notice of Assessment.

Current Year Estimate  Optional — complete prior years above first

⚠️ Important: With this box checked, payment recommendations where the Current-Year Option is optimal will be based on your estimate. If your actual tax liability turns out higher, CRA will charge interest on the shortfall. If you do not want any risk of being wrong, uncheck this box — the calculator will use only the No-Calculation and Prior-Year methods, both grounded in your actual filed tax amounts.

$
%
This input only impacts periods after June 30, 2026. Actual historical prescribed rates are built into the math for earlier timelines.
Payments Made or Planned

Enter payments already made or planned future payments. Credits are calculated from each payment date to April 30, 2027.

Pre-filled with the standard CRA due dates. Change any date, adjust amounts, or add additional payments to model your actual or planned schedule.

DateAmount ($)

Enter your figures and click
Calculate Instalments to see results.

Notice Details

CRA Instalment Notice
Assumptions
This is the year your income was earned — not the year you file. For example, instalments made in 2026 typically relate to your 2026 tax year.
Filing & payment deadline: Instalment interest is calculated to April 30, 2027 for all individual filers — including self-employed individuals whose filing deadline is June 15.
%
This input only impacts periods after June 30, 2026. Actual historical prescribed rates are built into the math for earlier timelines.
Amounts from Your Notice
$
CRA sets Q1 and Q2 at the same amount — this figure applies to both March 15 and June 15.
2025 Notice of Assessment
Why we need this: Your March/June notice covers Q1 and Q2 only. With your prior year net tax, we can calculate Q3 and Q4 right now — giving you the complete annual picture without waiting for the August notice.

Where to find it: Use your most recently filed T1 return or Notice of Assessment — your NOA is preferred since it is the official CRA-confirmed figure. Enter the four line numbers below. If you don’t have your NOA handy, your T1 return will also work.

Enter the figures from your most recently filed T1 return or Notice of Assessment. Most people only have lines 43500 and 43700 — enter 0 for any line not shown on your NOA.

From Your March & June Notice
$
The amount shown on the notice you received in February — same for both March 15 and June 15.
From This Notice
$
CRA sets Q3 and Q4 at the same amount — applies to both September 15 and December 15. Enter $0 if this notice shows nothing owing.
2025 Net Tax Payable  Recommended
Why this matters: Your Q1 and Q2 amounts were set using your second-prior year tax. CRA’s Prior-Year Option — which uses your most recently filed return — may require significantly different amounts. This is especially important when Q3 and Q4 are $0, which typically means your prior year tax was low enough that the formula produced zero — and the Prior-Year Option may have been cheaper than what your March/June notice requested.

Where to find it: Your most recently filed T1 return or Notice of Assessment (NOA preferred — it is the official CRA-confirmed figure) — look for the following four line numbers and input them below. Most people only have lines 43500 and 43700 — enter 0 for any line not shown on your NOA.

Enter 0 for any line not shown on your NOA or T1.

Payments Made or Planned

Pre-filled with standard CRA due dates. Change any date, adjust amounts, or add additional payments — each entry earns its own interest credit based on how many days before April 30, 2027 it was made.

DateAmount ($)
Current Year Estimate  Optional

⚠️ Important: With this box checked, any payment recommendation where the Current-Year Option is optimal will be based on your estimate. If your actual tax liability is higher than estimated, CRA will charge interest on the difference. If you do not want any risk of being wrong, uncheck this box — the calculator will use only the No-Calculation and Prior-Year methods, both grounded in your actual filed tax amounts.

$

Enter the amounts from your CRA notice
and click Show Payment Options.

Important Disclaimer: This calculator is provided by TIER Wealth for general educational and planning purposes only. It is not intended as tax advice and does not replace the advice of a qualified tax professional or the Canada Revenue Agency. Results are estimates only — actual instalment obligations, interest, and penalties are determined by CRA based on your specific tax situation. Interest calculations use daily compound methodology consistent with CRA’s approach; minor rounding differences may occur. Always confirm prescribed interest rates, instalment amounts, and filing deadlines directly with CRA or your accountant. TIER Wealth assumes no liability for decisions made based on these estimates.
